name: Iowa Chop with Apricot Pecan Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Packaged seasoned bread, stuffing crumbs
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried apricots in brandy and water for 30 minutes.
2. Drain the apricots, reserving the liquid, and chop them finely.
3. Place the chopped apricots in a large bowl.
4. Sauté onions in butter until tender. Add to the apricots and reserved liquid; set aside to cool slightly.
5. In a separate bowl, combine the remaining stuffing ingredients (except the bread crumbs) in the order listed. Mix well to break up the eggs.
6. Add the bread crumbs and mix well. Refrigerate the stuffing while preparing the pork.
7. Make a pocket in each pork chop by slicing horizontally toward the bone halfway up the thickness of the chop.
8. Stuff each chop with about 1/3 cup of the prepared stuffing.
9. Sear both sides of the chops in a skillet with vegetable oil.
10. Arrange the chops in a baking pan, cover, and bake for 35-40 minutes at 350°F (175°C) until the pork reaches an internal temperature of 160°F (71°C).
11. Serve the remaining stuffing as a side dish. Place it in a greased baking dish and bake uncovered at the same temperature.
12. To flambé the meat, heat brandy in a small saucepan until hot but not boiling. Carefully pour the hot brandy over the hot baking pan with the meat and ignite with a lighted match. The flames will subside after a minute or so. Serve the chops immediately on a heavy trivet.
